title: Trump announces new pick for surgeon general nomination
description: President Trump announced a third nominee for surgeon general, Dr. Nicole B. Saphier, a regular Fox News contributor, after pulling his previous nomination for Dr. Casey Means.

title: Congress ends record shutdown at the Department of Homeland Security
description: The House voted Thursday to end a record-breaking agency shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security.

title: Myanmar attempts to rehabilitate image with Suu Kyi move
description: State television in Myanmar says detained opposition leader Aung San Suu Kyi has been transferred from prison to house arrest, more than five years after the military coup that removed her from power.

title: Prosecutors release video of armed man storming correspondents' dinner
description: Federal prosecutors released a video Thursday showing the moment authorities say a man armed with guns and knives tried to storm the White House Correspondents' Association dinner and attempt to kill President Donald Trump.

title: Trump drops MAHA favorite for surgeon general pick who has critiqued RFK Jr.
description: Nicole Saphier, Trump’s third pick for the role, is a radiologist and longtime Fox News contributor. Means’s nomination stalled as some Republicans questioned her credentials and stance on vaccinations.

title: Louisiana suspends House primaries as red states face pressure to redistrict
description: Gov. Jeff Landry (R) issued an order pausing next month’s primaries until lawmakers can approve a new map, which could help the GOP gain one or two seats in the state this fall.

title: Senators ban themselves from participating in prediction markets
description: The platforms, which have gained scrutiny recently, allow people to place bets on future events such as election results. The ban also applies to Senate staffers.

title: Tracking who Trump is appointing to fill key administration roles
description: Follow President Trump’s progress filling over 800 positions, among about 1,300 that require Senate confirmation, in this tracker from The Washington Post and the Partnership for Public Service.

title: Hegseth argues Iran ceasefire ‘pauses’ deadline for Congress’s approval
description: The War Powers Act requires that the Trump administration seek lawmakers’ consent to continue hostilities beyond 60 days, a time limit that expires Friday.

title: Mapping where the redistricting fight stands and where it’s headed
description: As Trump pushes for a more Republican-friendly House map, more than half a dozen states are potential targets for mid-decade tweaks to congressional boundaries.

title: Maine Gov. Janet Mills says she's suspending her campaign for U.S. Senate
description: Maine Gov. Janet Mills announced Thursday that she is suspending her campaign for U.S. Senate. That move shakes up a race Democrats see as critical in gaining the majority in the U.S. Senate.

title: The Comey indictment could be upended by this 2015 Supreme Court precedent
description: The high court a decade ago explicitly overturned the legal standard that prosecutors are now citing to charge Comey with threatening President Trump.

title: Speaker Johnson calls for redistricting Southern states before November
description: After the Supreme Court limited the Voting Rights Act, Johnson said states should consider redrawing House maps before the midterms. New boundaries would help the GOP.

title: On chaotic day, Johnson navigates multiple internal revolts in House
description: Over hours on and around the chamber’s floor, the speaker and fellow Republican leaders cajoled holdouts on a surveillance bill, a farm policy bill and funding for DHS.

title: Trump pulls Casey Means' stalled surgeon general nomination, announces new pick
description: President Trump says he's nominating former Fox News Channel contributor Dr. Nicole Saphier for surgeon general after Dr. Casey Means' path forward stalled in the Senate over questions about her experience and her stance on vaccines.

title: U.S. House primaries in Louisiana are suspended after Voting Rights Act ruling
description: Louisiana suspended its upcoming primaries for the U.S. House, following Wednesday's U.S. Supreme Court ruling that the state's congressional map is an "unconstitutional racial gerrymander."

title: Maine Gov. Janet Mills drops out of race to unseat Republican Sen. Susan Collins
description: The seat is critical to Democrats’ hopes of retaking the Senate. Graham Platner, an oyster farmer and Democrat, is likely to win the primary.

title: Congress ends record shutdown at the Department of Homeland Security
description: Thursday's vote in the House provides funding for DHS after a more than two-month shutdown,  but does not include dollars for Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Customs and Border Protection.

title: Trump, Iran are locked in high-stakes standoff as oil prices hit 4-year high
description: As the U.S. blockade and Iranian mines and drones halt shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. gas prices also reached a level not seen since just after Russia’s Ukraine invasion.

title: 'Fool me once…' Lawyers argue Kennedy Center should not meet same fate as the East Wing
description: Lawyers made arguments in hearings for two separate lawsuits against President Trump and the Kennedy Center's board this week. Both lawsuits want to halt plans to close the performing arts venue for two years for renovations.

title: Maine Gov. Janet Mills suspends her U.S. Senate campaign
description: The move likely sets up a battle this fall between incumbent Republican Sen. Susan Collins and progressive Democrat Graham Platner, who is closely aligned with Vermont Sen. Bernie Sanders.

title: Public rejects Trump’s ballroom by wide margin
description: The president’s planned 250-foot arch and a Treasury Department plan to put Trump’s signature on paper money are also unpopular ideas.

title: Supreme Court decision could deliver GOP a host of House seats in 2028
description: While it’s too late for many states to redraw congressional maps for the 2026 midterms, the court’s ruling to limit the Voting Rights Act could result in fewer Black members. “We’ve been dealt a bad hand,” says Georgia lawmaker.

title: Comey’s lawyers say they will challenge threat charges as vindictive
description: The former FBI director made his first court appearance after being indicted over a 2025 social media post that prosecutors say was a threat to the life of the president.

title: Jury convicts Afghan man of terrorism, doesn’t link him to deaths at Kabul airport
description: Jurors convict the man on a charge of conspiring to provide material support to an ISIS group, but it’s a rare setback in a high-profile terrorism case.

title: Supreme Court limits key provision of the landmark Voting Rights Act
description: The decision could touch off a scramble by Republicans to redraw majority-minority congressional districts, especially in the South, that could cost many Black Democrats their seats.

title: Hegseth spars with lawmakers as Iran war’s price tag hits $25 billion
description: In a fiery House meeting on the military budget, the defense secretary called lawmakers critical of the administration’s handling of the conflict “defeatist.”

title: What the Supreme Court did to the Voting Rights Act
description: Southern lawmakers can now draw congressional maps to break up minority voters, as the conservative majority continues to roll back decades-old civil rights protections.

title: Supreme Court wrestles with Trump effort to end temporary protections for migrants
description: The conservative justices appeared sympathetic to the administration’s contention that it can cancel humanitarian protections for Haitian and Syrian nationals.

title: Trump’s $1 million ‘gold card’ visa has not been as popular as advertised
description: Just 165 people have paid the application fee so far, the government said in a new legal filing.

title: Pam Bondi agrees to testify before Congress on Epstein files, committee says
description: The former attorney general had been subpoenaed to appear at a closed-door session. When she was fired by Trump, her appearance was thrown into limbo.

title: Florida lawmakers approve redistricting plan intended to give GOP four more seats
description: Republican state legislators drew new districts that are expected to be challenged in court.

title: Supreme Court says antiabortion center can fight subpoena for donors’ names
description: Advocacy groups across the political spectrum aligned with the faith-based center in its battle with New Jersey’s attorney general over disclosing its donors.

title: Prosecutions of Trump’s foes add to GOP’s headaches in midterms
description: Charges against former FBI director James B. Comey and others take focus away from the economy and the issues voters care most about, many Republicans say.

title: Roots of political violence aren’t as simple as Trump makes them sound
description: Social upheaval, deep polarization and social media are primary drivers of the phenomenon, which has touched Republicans and Democrats alike.
